data_contracts_note: TOML structure is shown in examples but no formal schema or field definitions provided

PRESENT: Clear problem statement: end-of-line comments are lost when removing dependencies
Concrete reproduction case (MRE) with before/after TOML
Real-world example from astropy PR with link to diff
Root cause identified: toml_edit stores trailing comments in prefix of next item
Expected behavior: comments should be preserved
Reference to related issue (#16719) and closed issue (#18555)
PARTIALLY_PRESENT: Acceptance criteria: MRE shows desired output but no formal test cases or Given/When/Then
Scope: issue is about dependency removal, but unclear if this affects other list-removal scenarios (optional dependencies, dev dependencies, etc.)
Data contracts: TOML structure shown but no detail on toml_edit's comment storage mechanism or internal data model being fixed
ABSENT: Error states: No mention of what happens if comment format is malformed, if there are nested comments, or edge cases (multi-line comments, etc.)
Boundary conditions: What if last item has a trailing comment? What if comment is on removed item itself vs previous item?
Implementation detail: Which function/module in codebase needs fixing (only mentions toml_edit behavior)?
Test cases: No explicit list of test scenarios or edge cases to validate
Performance implications: No mention of whether this fix has performance impact

dependency_context_notes: References specific file (crates/uv/src/commands/help.rs line 136) and pager binaries (less, busybox less, more) but doesn't detail API/function signatures being modified
outcome_clarity_notes: Clear that the fix addresses BusyBox less incompatibility with -P flag and NO_COLOR handling. However, the exact behavioral change (what flags to use instead, how NO_COLOR affects output) requires reading the code or inferring from context
error_states_notes: Mentions the specific error (unrecognized option: P in BusyBox) but doesn't describe what happens if pager selection fails, if NO_COLOR env var is malformed, or other edge cases
scope_boundaries_notes: Clear this is about help command paging. Scope limited to pager invocation and NO_COLOR environment variable. Doesn't specify if this affects other commands using pagers
acceptance_criteria_notes: Test plan mentions manual verification against specific versions (less 679, busybox v1.34.1) and testing with/without NO_COLOR, but no explicit pass/fail criteria or automated test cases documented
behavioral_specificity_notes: Specific about the problem (BusyBox less doesn't support -P flag) and versions tested, but vague on the solution (what flags are actually used now, how NO_COLOR changes the prompt styling)

data_contracts_notes: No explicit definition of escape syntax or format specification. The PR says 'respect escapes' but doesn't define which escape sequences are valid (backslash, quotes, etc.)

CONTAINS: Clear problem statement: --env-file option cannot handle paths with spaces on Windows (PowerShell) and Linux (bash)
Reproduction steps on two platforms (Windows/PowerShell and Linux/WSL) with concrete examples
Current behavior clearly demonstrated: paths with spaces fail, paths without spaces work
Evidence that --directory flag handles spaces correctly (showing capability exists in codebase)
Multiple attempted escape syntaxes that don't work (single quotes, backticks, double quotes, nested quotes)
Root cause identified: Clap doesn't support escaping, manual splitting needed
Solution approach: implement custom splitting logic that respects escapes
MISSING: NO specification of which escape character(s) should be supported (backslash? quote? both?)
NO definition of escape syntax for the end user (e.g., 'should users write --env-file="path with spaces/.env" or --env-file=path\ with\ spaces/.env?')
NO explicit error handling specification (what if escape sequence is malformed?)
NO acceptance criteria with concrete test cases (e.g., 'Given --env-file="dir with spaces/.env", then file should be found')
NO specification of behavior on edge cases (trailing escapes, consecutive spaces, multiple escape types, etc.)
NO data contract for the escape format (is it shell-style escaping? Python string escaping? Custom?)
NO specification of whether this applies only to --env-file or other space-delimited options
NO mention of backward compatibility concerns
NO specification of platform-specific behavior (does escaping work the same on Windows and Linux?)
NO mention of documentation/help text changes needed
NO scope clarification: does this touch environment variable parsing itself, or just file path resolution?
